message:
fix(reservations): use IMMEDIATE transactions to prevent stale WAL snapshots in conflict detection

Two related bugs where pooled SQLite connections re-used stale WAL read
snapshots, causing file reservation conflicts to be silently missed:

Bug #85 — get_active_reservations could return stale data after another
agent released a reservation, leading to phantom conflicts.

Bug #86 — the tool-layer conflict index check could pass with outdated
data, then create_file_reservations would correctly detect the conflict
at the DB layer but surface it as an opaque MCP error instead of a
structured conflict response.

Changes:
- queries.rs: wrap get_active_reservations in BEGIN IMMEDIATE to force a
  fresh WAL snapshot; add proper rollback on all error/cancel/panic paths
  and commit the read-only tx promptly to release the write lock
- reservations.rs: catch ResourceBusy errors from the DB layer, re-read
  active reservations with a fresh snapshot, rebuild the conflict index,
  and return structured conflict responses instead of propagating errors
- Cargo.lock: beads_rust 0.1.35 → 0.1.36

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
